commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
This day: shortly I must die. (Calmet)
Met am properamus ad unam. (Horace) "We hasten to one common goal. "(Haydock)
The pagans called death, or the grave, the common place; and Plautus says, in the same sense, Quin prius me ad plures penetravi. (Calmet) "Before I penetrate the receptacle of the many. "(Haydock)
Mind. Hebrew, "you know in your hearts, and in all your souls "you are convinced, you cannot be ignorant that God has fulfilled his engagements. (Calmet)
The Septuagint read, "you shall know "The experience of future ages will only establish this truth more fully. (Haydock)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
